key_insight: The paper derives the first complete analytical vector-geometry model for muon-ring light in dual-mirror Cherenkov telescopes, capturing effects from both the secondary mirror and camera obscuration.
engineering_relevance: This improves CTAO calibration by enabling more accurate optical throughput and point-spread-function monitoring, especially for Schwarzschild-Couder telescope designs with significant shadowing.
method: Formulate muon-photon and telescope geometry with vector algebra.
Use symbolic manipulation and Taylor expansions in SageMath to derive closed-form terms.
Validate against known single-mirror solutions and physically expected limiting cases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical validation plus visual parameter sweeps, with shadowing effects causing deviations of up to 40% from previously used methods.
watch_item: The model’s accuracy depends on geometric assumptions and careful treatment of baffles, mirror curvature, and limiting-case approximations in real telescope layouts.
paper_title: Using Muon Rings for the Calibration of the Cherenkov Telescope Array: An Analytical Solution for the Dual-Mirror Telescope Using Vector Geometry
